stem: A program uses both variables and constants to store values.
model_answer: A variable is a named location in memory whose value can change while the program is running (e.g. a score that updates during a game). A constant is a named location whose value is fixed when it is declared and cannot be changed while the program runs (e.g. VAT_RATE). Using constants makes code easier to read and maintain because the name explains the purpose and the value only has to be changed in one place.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Compare a variable and a constant.

mark_points.text: a variable's value can change while the program is running
accepted_alternatives: variables can be reassigned at run time

mark_points.text: a constant's value is fixed at declaration and cannot be changed while running
accepted_alternatives: constants keep the same value throughout the program
